what: The published anchor and the benchmark
title: Benchmark: hsquared vs sommer and pedigreemm
text: Published REML estimates (Wilson et al. 2010): σ²= 3.3954, σ²e = 3.8286, h² = 0.470. values computed reproducing code next section. CI test asserts pure-R reference sommer agree published anchor within signed-band (expect_equal(..., tolerance = 0.02), relative 0.02 variance component; h²-vs-sommer check absolute 0.02 bound). three agree roughly four decimal places. signed-band (maintainer, 2026-06-13; docs/design/01-v0.1-contract.md): variance components within ~1–2% relative, h² within ~0.01–0.02 absolute, EBV correlation > 0.999. CI test implements variance-component h² portion (relative 0.02 variance components via expect_equal); EBV-correlation criterion signed-target verified engine side, number R atom produces.

previous_headings: The published anchor and the benchmark
title: Benchmark: hsquared vs sommer and pedigreemm
text: exact code benchmark runs (gryphon anchor + optional sommer agreement leg):
code: library(hsquared)  # Gryphon birth-weight data (teaching data; Wilson et al. 2010) utils::data("DT_gryphon", package = "enhancer") DT <- DT_gryphon A <- A_gryphon dat <- DT[!is.na(DT$BWT), ] ids <- rownames(A)  # Published anchor pub <- c(sigma_a2 = 3.3954, sigma_e2 = 3.8286, h2 = 0.470)  # hsquared's independent pure-R REML reference on y, X, Z, Ainv y <- dat$BWT X <- matrix(1, length(y), 1L) j <- match(as.character(dat$ANIMAL), ids) Z <- matrix(0, length(y), length(ids)) Z[cbind(seq_along(j), j)] <- 1 ref <- hsquared:::hs_reml_estimate_reference(   y, X, Z, solve(A),   method = "REML", initial = c(sigma_a2 = 3, sigma_e2 = 4) ) # ref$estimate -> sigma_a2 = 3.3953, sigma_e2 = 3.8287; h2 = 0.470  # Two-sided agreement against sommer d2 <- dat d2$ANIMAL <- factor(as.character(d2$ANIMAL)) m <- sommer::mmes(   BWT ~ 1,   random = ~ sommer::vsm(sommer::ism(ANIMAL), Gu = A),   data = d2, verbose = FALSE ) # sort(unlist(m$theta)) -> 3.3954, 3.8286; animal h2 = 0.470

what: pedigreemm: a one-sided floor, not an agreement
title: Benchmark: hsquared vs sommer and pedigreemm
text: pedigreemm table , purpose. optimiser lands slightly REML optimum pedigree models, fit saturated one-record-per-animal design . run deliberately replicated dataset (three records per animal 12-animal Mrode pedigree, design non-degenerate), used assert one-sided lower bound: verified REML objective, hsquared’s solution least good pedigreemm’s. Executed result (replicated fixture): hsquared reaches higher (better) REML log-likelihood — logLik(hsquared) ≥ logLik(pedigreemm) — ~0.026. optimiser-quality floor, two-sided agreement, DGP recovery, ASReml parity.
code: fx <- hsquared:::hs_replicated_animal_comparator_fixture() lab <- as.character(fx$pedigree$id)  # pedigreemm REML fit on the replicated design ped <- pedigreemm::pedigree(   sire = match(as.character(fx$pedigree$sire), lab),   dam = match(as.character(fx$pedigree$dam), lab), label = lab ) dd <- fx$data dd$id <- factor(as.character(dd$id), levels = lab) m <- pedigreemm::pedigreemm(y ~ x + (1 | id), data = dd, pedigree = list(id = ped))  # hsquared reference + the shared REML objective; assert hsquared >= pedigreemm # (see tests/testthat/test-validation-fixtures.R for the full comparison).

title: Fitting quantitative-genetic models
text: two-effect model: additive-genetic effect plus independent IID environmental effect (example litter cage), grouped separate column.
code: fit_ce <- hsquared(   y ~ animal(1 | id, pedigree = ped) + common_env(1 | litter),   data = dat,   control = hs_control(     engine = "julia",     engine_control = list(target = "two_effect")   ) )  variance_components(fit_ce)     # animal, common_env, residual common_env_effects(fit_ce)

title: Fitting quantitative-genetic models
text: two-effect model: direct additive-genetic effect plus maternal genetic effect expressed dam. carry pedigree relationship (maternal effect uses ₂ = pedigree ), dams must animals animal() pedigree. independent (uncorrelated) direct–maternal model; correlated 2×2 G version planned.
code: fit_mat <- hsquared(   y ~ animal(1 | id, pedigree = ped) + maternal_genetic(1 | dam),   data = dat,   control = hs_control(     engine = "julia",     engine_control = list(target = "two_effect")   ) )  variance_components(fit_mat)    # animal, maternal_genetic, residual maternal_effects(fit_mat)

title: Reading G matrices
text: current two---trait Gaussian animal model, vec(𝐚)∼MVN(𝟎,𝐆⊗𝐀),vec(𝐞)∼MVN(𝟎,𝐑⊗𝐈). \mathrm{vec}(\mathbf ) \sim \mathrm{MVN}\left(\mathbf 0, \mathbf G \otimes \mathbf \right), \qquad \mathrm{vec}(\mathbf e) \sim \mathrm{MVN}\left(\mathbf 0, \mathbf R \otimes \mathbf \right). additive relationship matrix pedigree. G trait--trait additive-genetic covariance matrix. R trait--trait residual covariance matrix. two matrices scale response traits fixed effects formula.
code: fit_mv <- hsquared(   cbind(weight, length) ~ sex + age + animal(1 | id, pedigree = ped),   data = dat,   family = gaussian(),   REML = TRUE,   control = hs_control(     engine = "julia",     engine_control = list(target = "multivariate")   ) )

title: Reading G matrices
text: Use G_matrix() want additive-genetic covariance matrix language quantitative genetics. alias explicit genetic_covariance() extractor. Read diagonal additive-genetic variance trait. Larger diagonal entries mean additive-genetic variation trait’s measurement scale. Read -diagonal additive-genetic covariance traits. positive entry means animals higher additive-genetic values one trait tend higher additive-genetic values . negative entry means additive-genetic values tend move opposite directions. interpretation across traits different units, use genetic correlation: genetic correlation standardized, often easier read raw covariance. causal statement . correlation tells additive-genetic deviations line fitted model; selection response still needs relevant selection gradient clearly defined prediction target.
code: G_matrix(fit_mv) genetic_covariance(fit_mv) genetic_correlation(fit_mv)

title: Validation evidence
text: strongest correctness evidence known-truth recovery study following ADEMP framework (Morris, White & Crowther 2019) — standard checklist simulation studies: Aims, Data-generating mechanism, Estimands, Methods, Performance measures. Data simulated univariate Gaussian animal model known variance components clean, non-selfing generational pedigree (n = 420 animals; founders unrelated). True breeding values drawn u = sqrt(sigma_a2) * U' z = U'U, Cov(u) = sigma_a2 * . estimator must recover generating values produce EBVs track true breeding values. Recorded result (engine ai_reml, 120 replicates, fixed master seed 20240613, n = 420, converged): σ²ₐ, σ²ₑ, h² near-unbiased — 0 lies inside bias ± 2·MCSE three (e.g. h² bias −0.0049, MCSE 0.0073); EBVs track true breeding values, mean cor(EBV, true) ≈ 0.74 h² = 0.4; engine matches independent pure-R reference machine precision. Recovery holds across h² grid (0.2 / 0.4 / 0.6, 100 reps per cell): near-unbiased, EBV accuracy rising h² (≈ 0.60 / 0.74 / 0.83). near-boundary cell (h² = 0.1) reported honestly, hidden: mild upward bias, 94% convergence, 5% boundary pinning σ²ₐ (estimate hitting lower bound zero). Recovery also holds model fixed effect (y ~ x + animal): h² near-unbiased slope recovered (b_x ≈ 0.99 vs 1.0). full study reproducible evidence data-raw/dgp-recovery-study.R (.Rbuildignored, part build). faster pure-R regression test guards small-N case CI (test-validation-fixtures.R, “REML recovers known variance components simulated DGP”).

what: R Interface for Julia-Backed Quantitative-Genetic Models
title: R Interface for Julia-Backed Quantitative-Genetic Models
text: hsquared R interface open, Julia-backed quantitative-genetic modelling system. R package owns applied-user surface: formula syntax, data validation, summaries, extractors, examples, bridge HSquared.jl engine. Version 0.1 fits univariate Gaussian animal model y ~ fixed + animal(1 | id, pedigree = ped) REML HSquared.jl engine. default hsquared() call fits model returns heritability, variance components, breeding values (EBVs/BLUPs), fixed effects, fitted values, residuals, diagnostics. Fitting requires local Julia, JuliaCall package, HSquared.jl checkout; without default call errors install guidance, control = hs_control(engine = "validate") validates contract without fitting. Use model_spec() preview parsed fixed-effect design, sparse animal-effect design, normalized pedigree ordering, Julia targets without fitting. v0.1 fit validated known-truth recovery (replicated DGP study engine recovers generating variance components near-unbiased), published gryphon REML estimate (Wilson et al. 2010, engine recovers within maintainer-signed-comparator band), agreement sommer package. engine-recovery results validated locally R--Julia bridge require local Julia plus HSquared.jl checkout; public CI exercises equivalent pure-R REML reference skip-guards live-engine tests (Julia CI). fitted-object extractors — variance components, heritability, EBVs/BLUPs, PEV, reliability, accuracy, fixed effects, random effects, log-likelihood, AIC, prediction, fitted values, residuals, summary(), coef(), nobs(), fit_diagnostics() (including at_boundary flag) — operate fitted object. Experimental, asymptotic REML standard errors confidence intervals surfaced local engine provides (clearly labelled experimental, coverage-calibrated); validated SEs/CIs confint()/vcov() remain v0.1 scope. advanced control = hs_control(engine = "julia") path exposes explicit engine targets (supplied-variance Henderson MME, sparse REML). lightweight hs_data() container now records phenotype, pedigree, genotype, expression, marker, annotation, environment inputs future integrated workflows, including optional expression-feature annotation diagnostics annotation_id environment-key diagnostics environment_id. package also provides readable formula vocabulary genomic/QTL terms standard quantitative-genetic extensions. Several now fit opt-, experimental engine path (engine = "julia", REML-supplied-variance, default, mirroring partial validation gate): permanent environment, common environment, maternal-genetic, genomic (GREML SNP-BLUP), single-step effects, multivariate Gaussian animal model via cbind() response. rest — paternal effects, dominance, epistasis, cytoplasmic inheritance, imprinting, custom relationship precision matrices, marker/QTL scans — syntax reservations currently abort planned, implemented. Use formula_status() inspect parsed, reserved, planned formula grammar R. Output extractor names qtl_table(), gwas_table(), eqtl_table(), marker_variance_explained(), lod_scores() reserved future fitted marker/QTL/eQTL results; marker_effects() live opt-SNP-BLUP path. intended two-package shape : v0.1 fit univariate Gaussian animal model: fits default: R side builds y, X, sparse Z, normalized pedigree payload, HSquared.jl engine builds Ainv, estimates variance components REML, returns hsquared_fit object. genomic GREML / SNP-BLUP effect, single-step, multivariate Gaussian animal model, non-Gaussian (poisson/binomial, Laplace-REML, heritability) models also fit opt-, experimental engine = "julia" path (default); factor-analytic models remain planned. multivariate Gaussian path, use: multivariate path REML-, animal-model-, dense validation-scale, partial: returns G/R covariance correlation matrices, per-trait h2, cross-trait EBVs, yet ASReml-style production multi-trait claim t>=2 known-truth recovery claim. supplied-variance MME checks, use: validation bridge target supplied variances, optimizer. Current validation atoms include tiny deterministic Ainv checks, optional Mrode9/nadiv pedigree-Ainv comparator, supplied-variance Henderson MME fixture compares R reference fixed effects, EBVs, fitted values, h2, optional dense validation-path PEV/reliability Julia available, Mrode-style supplied-variance output fixture pins Ainv, fixed effects, EBVs, fitted values, PEV, reliability, h2, ML log-likelihood, dense/sparse REML log-likelihood. Optional local tests also compare Julia dense REML sparse REML likelihood evaluators tiny supplied-variance three-founder fixture. tiny supplied-variance deterministic validation fixtures — check engine arithmetic, estimation, reachable default call. Use validation_status() inspect validation evidence planned comparator lanes R. integrated workflows, parser can read phenotypes pedigree hs_data() bundle: Marker maps can also checked container: Expression feature annotations environment metadata can also checked without fitting eQTL, omics, environmental models: expression-feature, genotype-column, marker-map, annotation-feature, environment-key checks metadata validation . Genomic, QTL/eQTL, omics, environment-effect models remain planned. summary(bundle) data_status(bundle) report pedigree coverage, founder parent-link counts, marker-map size, genotype marker-column count, missing genotype value counts, unnamed duplicate genotype marker columns, chromosome count, coordinate range, whether genotype-marker alignment checked, expression row feature counts, unnamed duplicate expression features, expression-feature annotation coverage annotation_id supplied, environment metadata coverage environment_id supplied. genotypes markers supplied, genotype marker column names must match marker-map IDs exactly. animal-model parser uses bundle pedigree default, animal(1 | id) equivalent spelling animal(1 | id, pedigree = pedigree) data = bundle. interface rule deliberately simple: easy, easy, easy. Applied users gold; package make common quantitative-genetic model feel obvious exposes specialist machinery.
code: hsquared       R package: friendly modelling interface for applied users HSquared.jl    Julia package: sparse quantitative-genetic engine fit <- hsquared(   y ~ sex + age + animal(1 | id, pedigree = ped),   data = dat,   family = gaussian(),   REML = TRUE ) fit_mv <- hsquared(   cbind(weight, height) ~ sex + animal(1 | id, pedigree = ped),   data = dat,   family = gaussian(),   REML = TRUE,   control = hs_control(     engine = "julia",     engine_control = list(target = "multivariate")   ) )  genetic_covariance(fit_mv) genetic_correlation(fit_mv) heritability(fit_mv) breeding_values(fit_mv) fit_mme <- hsquared(   y ~ sex + age + animal(1 | id, pedigree = ped),   data = dat,   family = gaussian(),   REML = FALSE,   control = hs_control(     engine = "julia",     engine_control = list(       target = "henderson_mme",       variance_components = c(sigma_a2 = 1.2, sigma_e2 = 0.8)     )   ) ) spec <- model_spec(   y ~ sex + age + animal(1 | id, pedigree = ped),   data = dat,   family = gaussian() )  summary(spec) bundle <- hs_data(phenotypes = dat, pedigree = ped) summary(bundle) data_status(bundle)  spec <- model_spec(   y ~ sex + age + animal(1 | id),   data = bundle ) bundle <- hs_data(   phenotypes = dat,   pedigree = ped,   genotypes = geno,   markers = data.frame(     marker = c("m1", "m2"),     chr = c("1", "1"),     pos = c(10, 20)   ) ) bundle <- hs_data(   phenotypes = dat,   expression = expr,   annotation = genes,   annotation_id = "gene_id" ) bundle <- hs_data(   phenotypes = dat,   pedigree = ped,   environment = env,   environment_id = "site" )

title: Create hsquared control options — hs_control
text: engine Execution engine. "fit" (default) fits v0.1 Gaussian animal model via HSquared.jl engine; requires local Julia, JuliaCall package, HSquared.jl checkout. "validate" validates parser bridge payload without fitting, returns validated spec invisibly (confirming message). "julia" exposes advanced opt-bridge explicit target control. backend Planned compute backend. One "auto", "cpu", "threads", "cuda", "amdgpu", "metal", "oneapi". accelerator Planned accelerator preference. One "auto", "none", "gpu", "cuda", "amdgpu", "metal", "oneapi". precision Planned numeric precision. One "float64" "float32". save Planned fitted-object storage mode. One "minimal", "full", "tiny". engine_control named list engine-specific controls. current experimental Julia bridge recognizes julia_project, initial, iterations, target, variance_components. target selects Julia estimator engine = "julia" bridge runs; effect default engine = "fit" path. supported targets "fit_animal_model", "ai_reml", "sparse_reml", "henderson_mme", "repeatability", "two_effect", "genomic", "single_step", "snp_blup", "multivariate", described . engine = "julia" target, bridge defaults target = "fit_animal_model": surfaces Julia-owned HSquared.fit_animal_model() dense NelderMead optimizer, honouring REML flag. estimator default engine = "fit" path, runs validated average-information REML estimator (HSquared.fit_ai_reml(), one reached target = "ai_reml"). two paths target Gaussian animal model differ optimizer level, estimates can differ slightly. Reach validated estimator engine = "fit" (ordinary path) engine = "julia" target = "ai_reml". target = "henderson_mme" supplied-variance validation path requires variance_components named sigma_a2 sigma_e2 values. target = "sparse_reml" experimental, opt-validation path surfaces Julia-owned HSquared.fit_sparse_reml() REML-sparse optimizer; accepts initial (named sigma_a2/sigma_e2) iterations. default, production fitting, variance-component estimation claim public R interface. target = "ai_reml" exposes average-information REML estimator (HSquared.fit_ai_reml()) default engine = "fit" path uses, explicit initial iterations control. validated v0.1 estimator univariate Gaussian animal model; engine = "fit" default ordinary way reach . target = "repeatability" experimental, opt-path repeatability (permanent-environment) model. requires animal(1 | id, pedigree = ped) + permanent(1 | id) formula surfaces Julia-owned HSquared.fit_repeatability_reml() REML-optimizer (three-component initial sigma_a2/sigma_pe2/sigma_e2). REML , default, additive permanent-environment variances identifiable repeated records per individual. target = "two_effect" experimental, opt-path two-effect models. requires animal(1 | id, pedigree = ped) plus second random effect — common_env(1 | group) (IID common-environment effect) maternal_genetic(1 | dam) (maternal genetic effect carrying pedigree relationship) — surfaces Julia-owned HSquared.fit_two_effect_reml() REML-optimizer (three-component initial sigma_a2/sigma_c2/sigma_e2). REML default. target = "genomic" target = "single_step" experimental, opt-paths fit single effect whose relationship user-supplied inverse: genomic(1 | id, Ginv = Ginv) (genomic relationship inverse) genomic(1 | id, markers = M) (marker matrix engine turns genomic relationship), single_step(1 | id, Hinv = Hinv) (single-step relationship inverse). surface HSquared.fit_ai_reml(). REML default. target = "snp_blup" experimental, opt-, supplied-variance path SNP-BLUP / RR-BLUP marker-effect model. requires genomic(1 | id, markers = M) (raw marker matrix) variance_components named sigma_g2 (genomic) sigma_e2 (residual) values, surfaces Julia-owned HSquared.fit_snp_blup(). estimates per-marker effects (marker_effects()) per-individual genomic breeding values supplied variances; estimate variance components default. target = "multivariate" experimental, opt-path multivariate Gaussian animal model. requires cbind(trait1, trait2, ...) response animal(1 | id, pedigree = ped), surfaces Julia-owned HSquared.fit_multivariate_reml() REML-optimizer, returns G/R covariance matrices, genetic residual correlations, per-trait heritability, cross-trait breeding values. default remains partial validation claim t>=2 known-truth recovery external-comparator evidence committed. reserved genetic_structure control accepts "unstructured" current R bridge; "diagonal", "lowrank", "factor_analytic" remain planned structured-covariance Julia engine surface lands main R bridge tests committed. future rank control also reserved currently errors instead ignored.

title: ggplot2 visualizations for hsquared results — hsquared-autoplot
text: Available types autoplot.hsquared_fit(): "variance" (default) – horizontal forest variance components per-trait h^2, approximate 95% intervals (+/- 1.96 * SE) fit carries experimental standard errors. "breeding_values" – sorted caterpillar estimated breeding values, +/- 1.96 * sqrt(PEV) bands prediction error variances available, faceted trait multivariate fits. "g_matrix" – rotation-invariant genetic-correlation heatmap estimated G multivariate fits (correlations invariant factor rotation; raw loadings never plotted – ratified cross-lane convention). "reaction_norm" – random-regression fits, genetic-variance heritability trajectories across covariate (faceted). heritability trajectory carries caveat rr_heritability(): homogeneous residual permanent-environment term can overstate h^2(t) repeated-records designs. figure helpers deliberately modular (takes tidy data frame returns ggplot) can factored shared visualization package later.

text: ggplot2 visualization layer (autoplot()). New autoplot() methods render hsquared results ggplot2 figures, style brms/bayesplot ecosystem consistent sister packages drmTMB/gllvmTMB: autoplot(fit, "variance") draws variance-component per-trait h² forest experimental 95% intervals (± 1.96·SE, labelled asymptotic/REML); autoplot(fit, "breeding_values") draws sorted EBV caterpillar ± 1.96·√PEV bands (faceted trait multivariate fits); autoplot(fit, "g_matrix") draws rotation-invariant genetic-correlation heatmap G multivariate fits (raw factor loadings never plotted — ratified cross-lane convention); autoplot(fit, "reaction_norm") draws genetic-variance heritability trajectories random-regression fit; autoplot(gwas_result) draws Manhattan plot carrying uncalibrated-significance banner. Also new: hs_recovery_forest() known-truth recovery studies (bias ± 2·MCSE, intervals covering zero = detectable bias) exported theme_hsquared(). figures uncertainty-first helpers modular (takes tidy data frame returns ggplot) can factored shared visualization package later. figure also carries attr(p, "hsquared_meta") honest-status attribute (type / interval status / rotation status / notes), implementing §3 R-drawing contract cross-lane plotting architecture shared HSquared.jl (13-plotting-layer.md). ggplot2 now dependency; base-R plot() method unchanged. Experimental random-regression (reaction-norm) model. new opt-target surfaces Julia-owned HSquared.fit_random_regression_reml(): hsquared(weight ~ sex + animal(rr(age, order = 2) | id, pedigree = ped), data = long_records, family = gaussian(), REML = TRUE, control = hs_control(engine = "julia", engine_control = list(target = "random_regression"))). rr(covariate, order = k) left animal() bar fits k-coefficient normalized-Legendre polynomial within-individual covariate (default order = 2 = intercept + slope); covariate standardized [-1, 1] observed range bounds recorded extractors can re-standardize user-supplied = original scale. New extractors: rr_covariance() (k x k coefficient genetic covariance K_g), random_coefficients() (per-animal predicted Legendre coefficients), reaction-norm trajectories rr_genetic_variance(fit, =), rr_heritability(fit, =), rr_correlation(fit, =), rr_eigenfunctions(fit, =) (rotation-invariant eigen-decomposition K_g covariate functions — eigenvalues, proportion genetic variance explained, sign-canonicalized eigen-coefficients, eigenfunctions psi_j(t); live-verified == HSquared.rr_eigenfunctions() ~1e-15) — computed R K_g recorded basis, matching engine convention. grammar (rr(...) inside animal()) ratified Julia twin HSquared.jl#61 (normalized Legendre standardized t ∈ [-1, 1]; Kirkpatrick/Meyer/Schaeffer). Experimental, opt-, REML-, single-effect, univariate, dense/validation-scale; mirrors twin #54 engine. Heterogeneous residual variance permanent-environment term still planned — current homogeneous residual permanent-environment effect, rr_heritability() can OVERSTATE h^2(t) repeated-records designs (test-day, growth curves). Multivariate random regression combining rr() second random effect planned, implemented (#54). Experimental gwas(fit, markers) post-fit marker scan. Runs dense, supplied-variance, relatedness-corrected mixed-model (GLS) Wald marker scan fitted Gaussian animal model, reusing fit’s estimated variance components pedigree relationship (surfacing Julia-owned HSquared.mixed_model_marker_scan()). Returns per-marker table (effect, se, z, chisq, p_value, bonferroni_p, bh_qvalue, lod). p-values genome-wide calibrated — nominal Wald p-values plus Bonferroni/BH supplied markers , realistic-LD/design calibration, permutation, external comparator (engine gate HSquared.jl#48); wrapper applies one whole-pedigree relationship correction, although leave-one-group-(LOCO) scan exists engine-side (HSquared.loco_mixed_model_marker_scan()) R gwas() yet surface (R LOCO surfacing progress); print() restates . Verified live match engine element-wise. Experimental, dense/validation-scale; reserved tabular gwas_table()/qtl_table()/eqtl_table() extractors stay reserved planned map-annotated API (#23). G-matrix geometry / evolvability extractors (Hansen & Houle 2008) opt-multivariate fits: eigen_G() (genetic eigenstructure — variance per genetic axis + genetic principal components, reserved name now implemented), g_max() (leading genetic axis), mean_evolvability(), directional evolvability(), variance_along_gradient(), respondability(), conditional_evolvability(), autonomy(). rotation-invariant functionals estimated G (factor loadings), well defined multivariate fit need loading-rotation convention — agreed cross-lane convention structured-covariance bridge may surface (HSquared.jl#42/#55). Computed R genetic_covariance(fit) verified match engine’s evolvability.jl definitions live parity test; experimental, REML-, standard errors, carrying multivariate fit’s partial status (#55). Experimental, opt-non-Gaussian (GLMM) animal model. hsquared() now accepts family = poisson() family = binomial() (binary 0/1) animal(1 | id, pedigree = ped) hs_control(engine = "julia", engine_control = list(target = "nongaussian")), surfacing Julia-owned HSquared.fit_laplace_reml() marginal (Laplace) REML optimizer. returns latent-scale additive-genetic variance, breeding values, fixed effects, marginal log-likelihood. non-Gaussian family residual-variance scale, heritability reported (liability-scale h² unbacked claim). Experimental REML/Laplace-(marginal = "laplace"; variational binomial trial count planned); mirrors engine row V6-LAPLACE (partial): coverage-calibrated, external comparator, Bernoulli σ²prone search-bound boundary small scale. default (#44). REML-, asymptotic (logit delta-method) interval — mirroring engine row V1-HERIT-CI (partial), coverage-calibrated unreliable small n. reported point estimate plus bounds, validated capability (#11). Experimental: variance_component_standard_errors() heritability_standard_error() return large-sample (delta-method) standard errors REML average-information matrix, default Gaussian fit’s engine provides . V1-HERIT-CI (partial) caveats: asymptotic, REML-, omitted near variance-component boundary (ill-conditioned AI matrix), coverage-calibrated, validated capability. Added base-graphics plot() method hsquared_fit: type = "variance" plots variance components (experimental +/- 1.96 SE whiskers present) type = "residuals" plots residuals fitted values (#30). Added second published external-canon anchor: CI-runnable, Julia-free test pinning reference Henderson solver published Mrode (2014) Example 3.2 sire-model solutions (p.48), extending canon second model class (#32). "lowrank"/"factor_analytic" remain gated validated rotation convention. New covariance_structure_lrt(constrained, full) reports experimental diagonal-vs-unstructured likelihood-ratio test two multivariate fits data — statistic 2*Δloglik, df difference two fits’ genetic-covariance parameter counts (-diagonal genetic covariances, .e. t(t-1)/2 diagonal--unstructured case), interior null (boundary = FALSE), χ². mirrors V4-MV-REML (partial): asymptotic, REML-, validated test (#47). Validation evidence (multivariate, t = 2). Two reproducible studies now back experimental multivariate target (.Rbuildignored, part build). data-raw/multivariate-recovery-study.R 100-replicate cold-start known-truth recovery study six G0/R0 elements, genetic correlation, per-trait h² fall within bias ± 2·MCSE (100/100 converged; EBV accuracy 0.79/0.74) — detectable bias, agreeing twin’s 12-seed study (HSquared.jl#78/#79); cold-started identity, warm-start artifact. data-raw/multivariate-comparator-study.R full-unstructured-residual sommer external comparator reproduces engine’s serialized phase4_multitrait_parity G0/R0/β/h²/EBV ≤ 8e-5 additionally recovers -diagonal residual covariance -suite diagonal-residual sommer check . multivariate capability stays partial — evidence toward, promotion , twin-gated covered gate (#10).

Experimental REML-; correlated direct–maternal (2×2 G) model remains planned.
